description: IraGo Full-Product Transformation. Phase A (US-101..US-110) modernizes the admin console. Phase C (US-124..US-131, priorities 11-18, deliberately queued before Phase B) builds the Operator Company partner portal: IraGo is out of the pilot-management business - companies get admin-provisioned 'company' logins, a Partner Console (dashboard, their flights + which pilot flew each, self-managed pilots), price rules that actually price fares (immediate effect + audit), and profile changes gated behind an admin approval queue. Phase B (US-111..US-123, priorities 19-31) transforms the remaining surfaces: passenger booking (including the new Photon geocoding / landing-zone picker UI from commit d754779), operator console, auth + profile dialogs, and the marketing site. Plan + audit evidence: tasks/prd-admin-dashboard-transform.md. HARD CONSTRAINTS for every story: (1) never rename/remove element ids; (2) never rename CSS classes referenced by js/app/*.js - grep before touching; (3) never change inline onclick/onkeydown wiring UNLESS the story explicitly authorizes replacing it (some Phase B stories deliberately convert inline handlers to addEventListener for a11y - only where stated); (4) never edit js/app.bundle.js - edit js/app/*.js then run 'npm run build:js'; (5) bump the ?v= cache-buster on BOTH the css/app.css link and the app.bundle.js script in app.html whenever either changes; (6) no smart/curly quotes in JS; (7) SHARED-CLASS TRAP: .pd-stat, .pd-loading, .pd-error, .pd-breakdown, .pd-input, .profile-stats-grid, .op-section-title, .op-empty, .op-btn, .dashboard-pill, .op-status-badge, .admin-table, .admin-table-wrap, .admin-users-meta, .auth-error, .auth-field, .btn-auth-primary, .auth-btn, .admin-form-card, .admin-add-success are each used by MULTIPLE views - style via NEW classes or scoped selectors, never restyle a shared class globally; (8) HEADLESS environment: no browser, no screenshots - verify with static grep audits written to progress.txt; server-side stories may additionally verify by booting 'node server.js' and curling localhost; do NOT claim visual verification; (9) LINE NUMBERS DRIFT: these files are edited by successive iterations - every line hint in a story is 'at review time'; re-locate every anchor by function name/selector with grep immediately before editing; (10) APP/MARKETING WALL: app-side stories (every story EXCEPT US-121..US-123) must not touch index.html, contact.html, team.html, vision.html, css/tokens.css, css/components.css, css/layout.css, css/responsive.css, css/animations.css; US-121..US-123 must not touch app.html, css/app.css, or js/app/* (server.js additions allowed only where the story says so); Phase C stories touch src/ + server.js by design - follow the existing idempotent schema pattern (src/schema.js) and never weaken the requireTailscale gate on /api/admin.

userStories.title: Landing-zone picker: fix flow integration and interaction defects (booking)
userStories.description: As a passenger, the new landing-zone picker (commit d754779) should be a deliberate refinement step, not a popup that fires on every location set, fights the auto-search flow, renders into hidden panels, and has dead map markers and keyboard-unreachable rows. This story authorizes replacing the picker's inline onclick wiring with addEventListener.
acceptanceCriteria: Intent gating: setPickup/setDest currently call showLandingPicker unconditionally (js/app/05-map.js, end of both functions) - the picker must NOT fire during the silent auto-GPS pickup on page load, and must NOT fire (or must be dismissed with its markers cleared) when #panel-locations is hidden because results are showing (searchRides hides it in js/app/06-booking.js)

Race fix: onRouteReady auto-calls searchRides when both coords are set - either gate that auto-search until a pending picker is resolved/skipped, or make searchRides cancel the pending Overpass fetch, remove the letter markers, and skip the picker's fitBounds so the map never yanks away from the just-drawn route; selecting a popular route (selectRoute sets both ends) must trigger at most ONE picker, not two 200ms apart

Re-open affordance: add a small 'Refine pickup/landing spot' chip on the confirmed location row that reopens the picker on demand (Uber pattern), so gating it does not strand the feature; evidence must show the chip handler actually calls showLandingPicker

Map markers become interactive: each landing-zone L.marker gets a click handler that selects the zone (the hover-sync pattern between list and markers already exists nearby in 05-map.js - follow it); list rows become <button> elements (or role=button + tabindex=0 + Enter/Space) so keyboard users can pick

Replace the inline onclick string wiring for rows: the current code builds onclick="selectLandingPoint(...,'name',...)" from escapeHtml'd names - escapeHtml already turns ' into ' which the HTML parser decodes back inside the attribute, so any OSM name with an apostrophe (e.g. St. Mary's Ground) is a JS syntax error and a dead row - wire via addEventListener with the zone index instead (explicitly authorized)

Display bug: the list is shown via list.style.display='block' while its CSS declares display:flex with gap 4px - the gaps are silently dropped; toggle visibility via the hidden attribute or a class so the flex layout applies

Feedback states persist: the empty ('No landing points found') and error ('Could not scan') states currently auto-dismiss the whole picker on 2.5-3s timers and the error string permanently overwrites the default empty copy - keep the state visible with the existing Skip button as the dismiss, and reset the empty-state copy on every invocation

All JS edits in js/app/05-map.js (+ 06-booking.js if the search gating needs it); app.html may be touched only for the ?v= cache-buster bumps and (optionally) the 'Refine' chip markup - the chip may instead be JS-injected; no element ids renamed; setPickup/setDest/selectLandingPoint/searchRides keep their names (adding an optional suppress/silent parameter to setPickup/setDest is authorized); npm run build:js; bump BOTH ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes

HEADLESS structural verification in progress.txt: quote the new guard conditions, the addEventListener row wiring, the marker click handler, the fetch-cancellation/marker-cleanup path, and quote the auto-GPS setPickup invocation showing the silent/suppress flag AND the guard inside setPickup/showLandingPicker that honors it (a bare 'no picker call in the geolocation callback' grep proves nothing - the call is indirect via setPickup)
userStories.notes: Intent gating (suppress param + panel-locations guard), race fix (searchRides cancels picker), refine chips, interactive markers, addEventListener (no inline onclick), display bug fixed (hidden attribute), feedback states persist (no auto-dismiss). Grep evidence in progress.txt US-111 entry.

userStories.title: Landing-zone picker + Photon autocomplete: design-system conformance and a11y
userStories.description: As a passenger, the new geocoding UI should speak the app's design language - it currently ships a foreign Tailwind palette, hardcoded radii/transitions, -apple-system font overrides, letter badges, and a dropdown with no ARIA and unstable results.
acceptanceCriteria: Re-skin all lp-* CSS rules onto tokens: var(--radius-md)/var(--radius-sm) instead of hardcoded 10px/8px/6px, var(--surface) instead of #fff, var(--border-subtle) instead of var(--gray-200) borders, var(--transition) instead of 'all 0.15s'; remove the -apple-system font-family on .lp-item-icon (body Inter cascades in the panel); .lz-marker-letter sits inside the Leaflet pane where leaflet.css sets its own font stack, so either delete the letter marker entirely per the SVG-icon criterion or set an explicit Inter font on it - do not rely on body cascade there

Category palette: LANDING_ZONE_CATEGORIES in js/app/05-map.js hardcodes Tailwind hues (#10B981, #22C55E, #3B82F6, #8B5CF6, #F59E0B, #EC4899) - replace with a palette derived from the app system (--blue #0057B8 family, greens, --amber family, navy tints; note --amber is itself #F59E0B, so a bare literal-for-var swap is not the point - the resulting set must read as the corporate palette, not Tailwind brights); category colors move into CSS classes consumed by both list icons and map markers (className on the Leaflet divIcon) with JS-injected hex remaining only where truly dynamic; the verification grep is SCOPED to LANDING_ZONE_CATEGORIES and the lp-*/lz-* CSS rules (drawRoute's hexes are out of scope for this story)
Replace the letter badges (H/P/S/K/G/R) with the app's stroke-SVG icon language + the existing .lp-item-cat text label (the letters have no legend anywhere and add nothing)

Photon dropdown stability: renderDropdown currently resets activeIdx=-1 and fully re-renders when the debounced Photon response lands, wiping arrow-key selection and shifting results under the cursor - patch/append the remote-results section into the existing DOM instead, preserve the active item across the update, and show a small inline loading row under 'Search results' while the fetch is in flight

Dropdown a11y (ARIA 1.2): role=combobox + aria-expanded + aria-controls + aria-activedescendant go on the INPUT itself; the dropdown gets role=listbox, items get role=option + unique ids; keyboard already works via the input's ArrowUp/ArrowDown/Enter handler - keep focus on the input and do NOT give items tabindex (the input blur handler hides the dropdown 150ms after focus loss, so tabbable items would close it before activation); converting item mouse wiring to addEventListener is authorized
No behavior regressions: debounce timing, local+remote merge order, and selection callbacks unchanged; setPickup/setDest signatures untouched

Honest liveness: the pulsing 'Live' dot in the rides header sits over a hardcoded static rideOptions array - either remove it or drive it from a real signal (e.g. currentNearbyOperators, populated by the feasibility call); when the signal is absent or the feasibility call fails, the badge must hide or render neutral - never a static pulsing dot; no fake liveness cues remain

alert() removal: useCurrentLocation falls back to a blocking alert() for unsupported geolocation - replace with the app's toast/inline error pattern

Popular routes survival: selectRoute currently destroys #popular-routes-area with innerHTML='' permanently - collapse the existing disclosure instead, and re-render the routes (renderPopularRoutes) in resetBooking/backToSearch so repeat bookings keep the shelf

Wiring preserved: searchRides/selectRideCard/bookRide/switchService/selectRoute keep names and callers (note: the ride-card handler is selectRideCard - there is no function named selectRide); all element ids survive; edits in js/app/06-booking.js + 05-map.js (+minimal app.html markup for the summary card)

userStories.title: Auth experience: desktop backdrop, motion, OTP inputs, form order, honest reset overlay
userStories.description: As a user signing in on desktop, the auth screen is a lone 420px card on 92% empty gray with zero motion, plain 14px OTP inputs, optional demographics blocking the essential path, and a forced-reset overlay dressed as a payment success.
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop backdrop (pick ONE and note the choice): (a) at >=1024px a split layout - left ~45% brand panel (navy gradient on tokens, tagline, subtle route-line/cloud motif - js/canvas-clouds.js and js/canvas-hero.js exist unused) with the existing card centered in the right 55%; or (b) keep the centered card but add a low-opacity aurora/mesh blob + faint dotted flight-path SVG behind it and strengthen the card's border/shadow; either way the auth card markup and all ids are untouched

Motion: an authCardIn entrance (~180ms opacity+translateY, reduced-motion safe) applied on card swaps via hideAllAuthCards() plus EVERY card-show path - the show*Card helpers AND the direct display writes in showRoleRegister and backToRegisterFromOtp, which bypass the helpers today; this story authorizes adding the class-toggle lines

OTP inputs: the FOUR 6-digit code inputs - #otp-code (signup-verify card, submit doVerifySignup), #reset-otp (reset card, submit doResetPassword), #reg-email-otp (inline register verify, submit regCreateAccount), #google-phone-otp (Google phone card, submit googlePhoneVerify) - get an .otp-input treatment (24px centered tabular digits, wide letter-spacing) and auto-submit at 6 digits where safe; EXCEPTION #reset-otp: doResetPassword also requires the new password, so auto-ADVANCE focus to #reset-password instead of submitting (auto-submit there fires the 'Enter the code and a new password' error)

Busy buttons: setBusy() swaps textContent only, so auto-width buttons visibly change size mid-press - pin min-width from offsetWidth and add an .is-busy treatment; CAUTION: setBusy has ~23 call sites across auth, operator, and admin views, and any injected child element would be wiped by the next textContent write - use a CSS-only spinner (::after on .is-busy) rather than DOM injection, and verify non-auth call sites still render correctly

Register form order: essentials first - Name, Email, Password; Gender/Age move below the password (one two-column row) or behind an 'Add optional details' disclosure; no field ids change, only markup order (payload building is id-based and reorder-safe - verify via regSendEmailOtp/regValidateFields in js/app/02-auth.js; doRoleSignup is the legacy path; there is no function named doRegister)

Must-reset overlay: the padlock glyph ALREADY exists - recolor its circle from success green to neutral blue via a SCOPED selector (#must-reset-overlay .confirm-check) or a new class, never globally (.confirm-check is shared with the booking-confirmation overlay #confirm-overlay); swap the placeholder-only inputs for the standard .auth-field label+input pattern with the existing .password-input-wrap show/hide toggle, and show the min-length rule as a static hint; ids and the submit handler unchanged

CSS debt cleanup: resolve the duplicate .auth-forgot-link definitions (the later wrapper-era block leaks margins onto the login card's inline button - scope or remove it); delete the earlier flex .profile-quick-stats block AND fold its still-effective margin-top:10px into the surviving grid rule (the grid rule does not set margin-top, so bare deletion shifts layout); remove the unused .auth-input rules (grep confirms zero usage) but leave .auth-btn UNTOUCHED - it is on the shared-class list, used by the admin Add Company form and .partner-add-fields scoped rules
Copy: setBusy call sites saying 'Sign In' become 'Sign in' to match the static markup and the rest of the auth sentence-case copy
npm run build:js; bump BOTH ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes

HEADLESS structural verification in progress.txt: quoted backdrop markup/CSS choice, quoted keyframe + reduced-motion guard, quoted .otp-input rule + auto-submit wiring, quoted reordered register fields with doRegister payload proof, quoted must-reset overlay structure, grep proof the duplicate/dead CSS blocks are resolved

userStories.title: Profile modal: tabbed IA, fixed hero geometry, differentiated badges, lazy loading
userStories.description: As any signed-in user, the profile modal is a 440px single column stacking 4-5 screen-heights of content, firing three fetches on every open, with a hero whose corners and margins don't fit the card and three role badges that all look identical.
acceptanceCriteria: Tabbed IA: a segmented control under the hero - Overview / Rides / Security - showing one pane at a time (COPY the .auth-role-switcher pill styling into NEW classes e.g. .profile-tabs/.profile-tab - do NOT reuse the .auth-role-btn class, which switchLoginRole queries document-wide and would strip the profile tabs' active state); Overview = quick stats + dashboard, Rides = ride history (fetch lazily on first tab visit instead of eagerly on open - authorized change to the open sequence; loadRideHistory's existing non-customer early-return stays intact), Security = change password + danger zone; 'Delete account' moves under Security behind a 'Danger zone' divider with destructive styling
Desktop width: the modal card grows from 440px to ~560px at >=600px (the single narrow column is a phone layout on desktop today)

Hero geometry fixes: top corner radius must match the card's (currently 14px hero inside a 24px card leaving white slivers), the mobile negative margins must match the mobile padding (currently hardcoded to desktop 24px causing 12px overflow), and the drag handle renders INSIDE the dark hero as a light pill instead of a white strip above it

Hero visual: navy gradient (tokens) + the navy-to-blue gradient avatar (precedent exists on the admin drawer avatar); badge differentiation REQUIRES JS wiring, not just CSS: syncProfileUI computes ROLE_PROFILE.dropdownClass ('role-admin'/'role-operator') but never attaches it - toggle it on the .profile-display-role badge, and toggle the 'verified' class with user.emailVerified (it is static markup today, so a green check tint would show for unverified users otherwise); THEN replace the three byte-identical rgba(255,255,255,.1) rules with green+check (verified), amber (admin), blue (operator) tints
Skeletons: profile dashboard/rides/user-drawer loading texts become shimmer skeleton shapes via NEW classes or scoped selectors (never restyle .pd-loading globally - it is shared)

All ids survive (profile-quick-stats, profile-dashboard, profile-rides, pd-refresh-btn, rides-refresh-btn, profile-display-* class contract); js-open-profile/js-close-profile wiring untouched; the tab control is new markup with new ids
npm run build:js; bump BOTH ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes

HEADLESS structural verification in progress.txt: quoted tab markup + lazy-load gating, quoted corrected hero geometry rules (desktop + mobile), quoted differentiated badge rules AND the syncProfileUI class toggles (CSS-only badge edits fail verification - the selectors currently match nothing), proof loadProfileDashboard + loadProfileQuickStats still fire eagerly in openProfileModal and loadRideHistory fires on first Rides-tab visit for customers (non-customer early-return preserved)

userStories.title: Marketing conversion: real contact form, responsive contact layout, dead links and placeholder content
userStories.description: As a prospective customer or partner, the contact form silently discards my message while telling me it sent, the emergency number is literally blank, footer links 404 into missing anchors, and the two-column layout crushes on phones. SCOPE: marketing files + server.js only - never app.html/css/app.css/js/app.
acceptanceCriteria: Real form submission: add a POST /api/contact route defined in server.js ITSELF (scope is marketing files + server.js - do not edit src/ files; express.json() is already installed in server.js and no /api/contact route exists anywhere); persist every non-honeypot submission to a contact_messages table created via an idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS run from server.js using the pool require()d from src/db.js (mirror the src/schema.js pattern without editing it), and additionally attempt an email notification by requiring createSmtpTransport from src/smtp-transport.js (see sendOtpEmail in src/email.js for the pattern) - if SMTP is unconfigured, still return success after the DB insert; contact.html's fake setTimeout success handler is replaced with a real fetch with distinct success/error states and an aria-live status region; honeypot field silently dropped server-side

Responsive contact layout: the inline grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr on .grid-split and the name-fields row move into a stylesheet class with stacking at <=768px (and <=480px for the name row) - grep confirms .grid-split currently has NO rule in any CSS file

Emergency contact: the 'Contact number:' lines that trail off into nothing (two places on contact.html) get a real tel: number if one exists in the repo/env, otherwise the row is removed until one exists - no blank placeholders shipped

Dead links: the contact footer's four /#anchor links (air-taxi, golden-hour, air-shuttle, operators) point at ids that do not exist - index.html's real section ids are home, about, problem, technology, why-irago, use-cases, impact, credibility, so repoint to /#use-cases or (preferred) replace the whole footer per the unification criterion; index.html's 'View All Use Cases' href='#' button is removed or pointed at a real target

Footer unification: contact.html's divergent old footer (different tagline, '(c) 2025 Irago', 'Platform Online') is replaced with the index.html footer content; the empty .footer-socials/.footer-bottom-links divs on index.html, vision.html, and contact.html are populated with real links or deleted (team.html has neither div - just confirm via grep)

Team page QA: resolve the ISHAAN RAO comment vs 'Ishant Rao' rendered-name mismatch to one consistent spelling (pick the rendered one unless the repo indicates otherwise, note the ambiguity for the founders in progress.txt)

VERIFICATION (this story is server-verifiable headlessly): node server.js boots clean; curl -X POST the new endpoint with a sample payload returns success and the row is retrievable via SELECT from contact_messages (MySQL is running locally - use the .env credentials); curl with the honeypot filled returns success but inserts NOTHING; quote both curl transcripts + the SELECT output in progress.txt; grep proof no /#dead-anchor links remain
npm run typecheck passes; app.html, css/app.css, js/app/* untouched (git status proof in progress.txt)

userStories.title: Marketing brand alignment: Inter, rounded shape language, real heroes, inline-style extraction
userStories.description: As a visitor moving between the marketing site and the app, I currently experience two brands: the site runs Helvetica/Arial with every radius zeroed and shadows removed while the app is Inter with rounded cards and soft shadows. SCOPE: marketing files only.
acceptanceCriteria: Typography: add the same Inter preconnect + Google Fonts link the app uses to all four marketing pages and switch --font-display/--font-body in css/tokens.css to the Inter stack - app.css defines its own fonts independently so this cannot affect the app

Shape language: port the app's radius scale into css/tokens.css (--radius-sm:8px/-md:10px/-lg:14px/-xl:18px/-2xl:24px, --radius-pill fully rounded) and replace --shadow-card:none with the app's layered rgba(15,23,42,...) recipes; the token flip alone is NOT enough - components.css hard-codes border-radius:0 in 11 selectors (.btn, .nav-link, .nav-contact-link, .btn-nav-cta, .navbar-drawer, .drawer-link, .check-icon, .hero-badge, .scroll-to-top, .footer-social-icon, .team-tag) plus inline page CSS (contact.html .form-control, team.html) - REPLACE each with the appropriate var(--radius-*) value (merely deleting the declaration leaves radius at its initial 0, not the token); grep proof: zero remaining 'border-radius: 0' literals across css/*.css (excluding css/app.css) and the four marketing pages

Sub-page heroes: replace the three flat-navy hero rectangles (team/vision/contact) with one shared .page-hero treatment - navy gradient + soft blue glow orb or faint route-line SVG pattern, one consistent h1 clamp (team and vision share clamp(36px,6vw,64px) while contact uses clamp(32px,4.5vw,52px) - unify all three on one) - implemented in components.css, referenced by all three pages

Off-palette cleanup: the Tailwind red rgba(239,68,68) badge/icon rules move to the existing --emergency token family, the select chevron's #A1A1AA data-URI is re-encoded with the --text-hint value, and the sky-blue rgba(74,144,217) aligns with --sky

Inline-style extraction: extract the repeated inline card-heading/stat/hero declarations into SHARED classes in components.css (.card-title, .card-text, .stat-value, .form-select etc.) - per-page <style> blocks do NOT count as extraction; measurable target: style= attribute count drops from ~89/62/33/25 (index/vision/team/contact) to under 20 per page; quote before/after grep -c counts AND the new class rules
Visual QA by static audit: no page references a removed class; every var() used exists in tokens.css; quote the changed token block and 2-3 representative converted components
app.html, css/app.css, js/app/* untouched (git status proof); npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Marketing performance, SEO and motion hygiene
userStories.description: As a visitor on a normal connection, the homepage ships ~7MB of unoptimized media with no lazy loading; shared links render with no preview; dead animation JS observes hundreds of nodes for zero effect. SCOPE: marketing files only.
acceptanceCriteria: Images: convert the three 1.7-1.8MB use-case PNGs and the 864KB hero PNG to WebP with sharp-cli, VERIFIED available offline in this environment (npx --offline sharp-cli -i in.png -o out.webp -f webp -q 80; cwebp and ImageMagick are NOT installed - do not claim they are needed); update the references; add loading='lazy' decoding='async' and explicit width/height attributes to every below-the-fold image; quote before/after file sizes in progress.txt

Hero video: preload='auto' becomes preload='metadata'; add a prefers-reduced-motion rule so the video hides and the poster shows (the current animations.css block zeroes animation/transition durations and hides canvas but never touches the video); keep autoplay muted loop for everyone else

SEO/social meta: every marketing page gets og:title/og:description/og:image/og:url, twitter:card=summary_large_image, and a canonical link; og:image is a 1200x630 crop generated from assets/images/hero-poster.jpg (it is 1280x720 - crop with sharp-cli, do not point at the poster as-is); canonical/og:url use https://irago.in/<page> on all four pages (derived from the info@irago.in address - no other domain exists in the repo; note the assumption in progress.txt); index.html additionally gets Organization JSON-LD; quote one rendered head block

Dead plumbing: either re-enable a tasteful scroll-reveal (opacity+12px translateY, reduced-motion safe - the JS already exists but CSS was force-disabled) or delete scroll-animations.js + the .reveal CSS and its script tags; fix or remove main.js's dead data-section nav-highlight block; replace the TWO filter:brightness(100) white-logo hacks (navbar .brand-logo and the footer logo img) with a real white asset (generate from irago-logo.png via sharp-cli tint or hand-author an SVG) while PRESERVING the two-state navbar behaviour: white logo over the transparent hero navbar, full-colour logo once .navbar.scrolled is set (see .navbar.scrolled .brand-logo { filter:none }) - e.g. two stacked imgs or a CSS background toggled by .scrolled; the always-dark footer uses the white asset directly
No regressions: all four pages still reference only assets that exist (grep every src/href against the filesystem); app files untouched (git status proof)
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Company role foundation: 'company' role, partner login portal, admin provisioning
userStories.description: As IraGo, we partner with operator companies who manage their own pilots - companies need their own login and admin-provisioned accounts. PRODUCT DECISIONS (locked): onboarding is admin-provisioned only (no public signup); the new role value is 'company'. This story is server + auth plumbing; the portal UI shell lands in US-125 (a placeholder #company-view is enough here).
acceptanceCriteria: Server role plumbing: add 'company' to ROLES in src/auth.js; add company:'company' to PORTAL_BY_DB_ROLE in src/role-login.js; mount router.post('/company/login', createRoleLoginHandler('company', ...)) in src/auth-routes.js mirroring the operator mount, plus 403 stubs for /company signup endpoints mirroring the operator loginOnly pattern; add /login/company (serves APP_HTML) and /signup/company (redirect to /login/company) in server.js next to the existing operator/admin portal routes

Client role plumbing in js/app/02-auth.js: SIGNUP_CONFIG gains a company entry (loginPath /api/auth/company/login, loginUrl /login/company, loginOnly/signupDisabled, its own loginTitle/Sub/Hint copy e.g. 'Partner Console'); parsePortalFromLocation regex extends to (operator|admin|company); portalForDbRole maps 'company'; routeForRole gains case 'company' -> showView('company-view') (+ the US-125 section bootstrap once it exists); apiFetch's 401-exempt regex extends to include company/login (otherwise a bad password triggers AUTH.clear() and a jarring view swap mid-form)
Client role plumbing in js/app/03-admin-profile.js: ROLE_BADGE/ROLE_PROFILE gain company entries; loginUrlForRole maps 'company' -> /login/company

Login screen: a fourth .auth-role-btn with data-role='company' label 'Partner' + switchLoginRole('company') wiring in #login-view; app.html gains a minimal
sibling (showView handles any .view id already)

Admin provisioning: CREATABLE_ROLES and FILTERABLE_ROLES in src/admin-routes.js gain 'company'; POST /api/admin/users REQUIRES a valid companyId when role==='company' (it is optional-when-present today - add the requirement server-side); #admin-add-role gains <option value='company'>Company account</option>; toggleAddMemberCompany shows the company select (office select hidden) for role==='company'; doAddUser's client-side role check accepts 'company' and requires the company selection; the existing mustResetPassword=1 insert + #must-reset-overlay forced-reset flow works unchanged for the new role (verify, do not modify)

Stats fall-through fix: buildProfileStats in src/profile-stats.js currently gives any unknown role customerStats - add a companyStats branch (company-scoped totals via the pilot-employer linkage: bookings b JOIN users u ON u.id=b.operatorId WHERE u.companyId = ?) so /api/me/stats returns company-shaped data, not passenger stats

requireAuth's getUserStatus SELECT only returns deletedAt/bannedAt/role - company routes need companyId, so either widen that SELECT/cache in src/auth.js (bust invalidateUserStatus semantics carefully) or re-query the users row inside company routes; decide, implement one way, and document the choice

WRONG_PORTAL behavior: a company user logging in at the passenger/pilot/admin portals gets bounced to /login/company (the PORTAL_BY_DB_ROLE addition makes this work - verify, and extend the WRONG_PORTAL auto-switch in doLogin if it whitelists roles)

VERIFICATION (server-verifiable headlessly): node server.js boots; create a company via POST /api/admin/companies and a company user bound to it via POST /api/admin/users (curl with an admin session or direct DB insert + bootstrap pattern); curl POST /api/auth/company/login succeeds and returns role 'company' + mustResetPassword true; login at the wrong portal returns 403 WRONG_PORTAL with portal:'company'; quote all transcripts in progress.txt; npm run build:js; bump BOTH ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes
userStories.notes: All criteria verified via curl against locally booted server. Company login, WRONG_PORTAL bounces, 403 signup stubs, companyStats, admin provisioning with required companyId, mustResetPassword flow all working.

userStories.title: Company portal shell + dashboard (Partner Console)
userStories.description: As an operator company, after logging in I want a real console - branded shell, grouped nav, and a dashboard summarizing my partnership with IraGo. Reuses the admin shell's CSS but with its OWN behavior classes (showAdminSection queries .admin-section/.admin-nav-item document-wide - reusing those class names for behavior would cross-wire the two consoles).
acceptanceCriteria: New module js/app/10-company.js (bundle concatenates js/app/*.js in numeric order via scripts/build-app-js.js; top-level scope is SHARED across modules - do not redeclare existing globals like AUTH/adminCurrentSection)

#company-view fills out: a booking-nav clone (logo, 'Partner Console' role chip styled like .admin-role-badge, .js-open-profile Profile button, /logout link, avatar #company-avatar filled like the other views), then an .admin-shell-style grid reusing the STYLING classes (.admin-shell/.admin-drawer/.admin-drawer-nav/.admin-nav-group-label/.admin-page-header/.adm-grid/.adm-span-*/.adm-kpi/.adm-skeleton are all role-agnostic CSS) but with behavior hooks on NEW names: sections carry class 'company-section' + id 'company-section-<name>' (hidden attribute convention, like admin - NOT the operator style.display convention), nav buttons carry data-company-section + onclick showCompanySection(name)

showCompanySection(name) in 10-company.js mirrors showAdminSection's contract - hides all .company-section (scoped: #company-view .company-section), un-hides the target, syncs .active on #company-view nav items, closes the drawer at <=768px, and lazy-loads per section - but every querySelectorAll is SCOPED to #company-view so the two consoles cannot cross-toggle

Nav groups: OVERVIEW (Dashboard), OPERATIONS (Flights, Pilots), COMMERCIAL (Price Rules), ACCOUNT (Company Profile) - Profile pinned bottom in the muted system-zone pattern; sections beyond Dashboard may render 'coming in the next stories' placeholders in THIS story only if their stories are not yet done (each later story replaces its own placeholder)

New server router src/company-routes.js mounted in src/api.js as router.use('/company', companyRoutes) - NOT under /admin (the admin mount is Tailscale-gated via requireTailscale; a partner portal must not inherit that); every route uses requireAuth + requireRole('company') and resolves the caller's companyId per US-124's chosen mechanism; a company user whose companyId is NULL or whose company is inactive gets a clean 403 with a friendly client state, not a crash

GET /api/company/dashboard returns company-scoped KPIs computed via the PILOT-EMPLOYER linkage (FROM bookings b JOIN users u ON u.id = b.operatorId WHERE u.companyId = ? - deliberately NOT b.companyId, which is the territory office set at creation and can disagree with who actually flew): completed flights (total + this month), gross revenue (SUM fareEstimate completed), net payout (SUM the persisted operatorPayout with per-row fallback to fareEstimate*(1-commissionRate) for legacy rows), pilots (total + on duty now), cancelled count; document the linkage choice in a code comment

Dashboard UI: routeForRole's company case bootstraps showCompanySection('dashboard'); KPI rows reuse admKpi()/ADM_ICONS on .adm-grid (.adm-span-3), skeleton loading via the .adm-skeleton pattern while fetching, welcome header 'Good day, <company name>' with the company code monogram; empty state for a brand-new company ('No flights yet - your pilots' completed IraGo trips will appear here')

VERIFICATION: curl /api/company/dashboard as a company user returns the KPI JSON scoped to that company only; a second company's user gets different numbers (seed via src/seed-operators.js companies + a demo booking, or document the SQL used to verify scoping); quote transcripts; structural grep evidence of the scoped querySelectorAll calls; npm run build:js; bump BOTH ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes
userStories.notes: All criteria verified via structural grep. Company shell with scoped showCompanySection(), dashboard KPIs via pilot-employer linkage, GET /api/company/dashboard endpoint, nav groups (Overview/Operations/Commercial/Account), placeholder sections for future stories, admKpi/ADM_ICONS reuse, CSS cache buster bumped. DB not available locally for curl verification but endpoint structure matches companyStats pattern.

userStories.title: Company Flights section: every flight flown by my pilots
userStories.description: As an operator company, I want to see the flights my pilots flew in partnership with IraGo - which pilot, route, when, status, fare, and my payout - in a modern filterable table.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/company/flights (src/company-routes.js): paged (limit/offset, newest first) bookings via the pilot-employer linkage (JOIN users pilot ON pilot.id = b.operatorId WHERE pilot.companyId = ?), returning per flight: id, createdAt/assignedAt, pickupName -> destName, service, status, distanceKm, fareEstimate, payout (persisted operatorPayout with the legacy fallback), pilot {id, name, aircraftType}, and customer FIRST NAME ONLY (privacy: a partner must not receive customer emails/phones - split the name server-side); optional query filters: status, pilotId, from/to date

Flights UI in #company-section-flights: page header + toolbar (status filter select, pilot filter select fed from /api/company/pilots or a distinct pilots-in-results list, date range optional), rows in the admin table language (divider-only rows, status chips reusing existing op-status-badge classes unrestyled, right-aligned tabular-nums fare/payout columns, pilot name with monogram avatar), pager in the US-103 style, skeleton rows while loading, filter-aware empty states ('No completed flights yet' vs 'No flights match these filters')
A summary strip above the table: flights count, gross, net payout for the CURRENT filter selection (computed server-side alongside the page, not client-summed from one page)

Payout column honesty: rows display the persisted bookings.operatorPayout when present; legacy completed rows without it show the computed fallback marked with a subtle 'est.' hint - never silently mix the two as if identical
Cross-company isolation: requesting with pilotId belonging to another company returns an empty/403 result, never leaks; write the curl proof

VERIFICATION: curl the endpoint as company A and company B users showing disjoint scoped results + the pilotId isolation test; quote transcripts in progress.txt; npm run build:js; bump BOTH ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GET /api/company/flights with pilot-employer linkage, paged results, status/pilot/date filters, cross-company isolation, customer first-name-only privacy, payout honesty (est. hint for legacy rows), summary strip (server-computed), UI with admin table language, op-status-badge chips, skeleton loading, filter-aware empty states, US-103 style pager. DB not available locally for curl verification but endpoint structure verified via server boot + grep evidence.

userStories.title: Company Pilots section: companies manage their own pilots
userStories.description: As an operator company, I manage my own pilots (IraGo is out of the pilot-management business): I can see my roster, add pilots, and deactivate them - directly, without admin approval (PRODUCT DECISION locked; admin retains full oversight via the existing Users section).
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/company/pilots: the company's pilots (users WHERE role='operator' AND companyId = mine) with name, email, onDuty, gpsUpdatedAt (freshness), aircraftType/aircraftReg, bannedAt, per-pilot completed-trips count + net payout subtotals (persisted-payout rule from US-126)

POST /api/company/pilots: creates a pilot account mirroring the admin add-user flow (validate name/email/password >= 6, 409 on duplicate email, mustResetPassword=1) with companyId FORCED to the caller's company (any client-sent companyId ignored), optional officeId validated to belong to the same company (offices via a new GET /api/company/offices reusing the regional_offices-by-company query pattern)

PATCH /api/company/pilots/:id/status: deactivate/reactivate by setting/clearing bannedAt - ONLY for pilots whose companyId matches the caller (404 otherwise, no existence leak); a deactivated pilot cannot log in or receive dispatches (bannedAt is already enforced by requireAuth's getUserStatus and dispatch's candidate query - verify and quote both, do not reimplement); call invalidateUserStatus(userId) after the flip so the 30s auth cache does not keep a deactivated pilot alive

Pilots UI in #company-section-pilots: roster rows (monogram, name+email stack, duty pill with GPS freshness hint, aircraft, trips/payout columns, Deactivate/Reactivate action with confirm), '+ Add pilot' action in the section header opening an add-form card cloned from the admin Add Member language (no company select - fixed to own company; office select from /api/company/offices), success shows the temp-password note ('the pilot must change it on first login'), skeletons + empty state ('Add your first pilot to start flying with IraGo')
Admin oversight unchanged: the admin Users section still lists ALL pilots with company chips (no behavior change; grep proof loadAdminUsers untouched)

VERIFICATION: curl transcript - company A creates a pilot (login works with forced reset), A deactivates it (pilot login then fails / duty rejected), A attempts PATCH on a company-B pilot id and gets 404; quote all in progress.txt; npm run build:js; bump BOTH ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GET /api/company/pilots with pilot-employer scoping, POST /api/company/pilots with companyId forced + mustResetPassword=1, PATCH /api/company/pilots/:id/status with cross-company isolation (404) + invalidateUserStatus, GET /api/company/offices, UI with roster rows (monogram, duty pill, GPS freshness, aircraft, trips/payout, deactivate/reactivate), add-pilot form with office select, skeletons + empty state. Admin Users section untouched (grep proof). DB not available locally for curl verification but server boots clean and endpoint structure verified.

userStories.title: Company price rules: real rate cards that price real fares (immediate effect + audit)
userStories.description: As an operator company, I configure my per-service price rules on IraGo and they actually price flights - not a decorative form. PRODUCT DECISION (locked): rule changes take effect immediately with an audit trail visible to admin (no approval gate). Honesty requirement from the pricing audit: rates must flow through feasibility -> booking -> quote -> pay identically, and dispatch must prefer the priced company, or the rules are fake.
acceptanceCriteria: Schema (idempotent, in src/schema.js initSchema following the existing CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + ensureColumn conventions - scripts/db-init.js just calls initSchema): company_service_pricing (id, companyId, service VARCHAR(64), baseFare DOUBLE, perKm DOUBLE, active TINYINT(1) DEFAULT 1, updatedAt, updatedBy, UNIQUE KEY (companyId, service)) and company_pricing_changelog (id, companyId, actorName, changes TEXT, createdAt) mirroring pricing_changelog

GET/POST /api/company/pricing (company-routes): shows platform defaults (SERVICE_PRICING taxi 500+200/km, golden 5000+600/km, shuttle 500+80/km) beside the company's overrides per service; POST validates numerics and clamps each override to 0.5x-3x of the platform default (server-side; reject outside with a friendly error), writes the row, and inserts a human-readable diff line into company_pricing_changelog exactly like POST /api/admin/pricing does for pricing_changelog

Pricing engine hook in src/pricing.js: a loadCompanyPricing(companyId, service) with the same 60s cache pattern as loadPricingConfig; estimateFare/fareBreakdown accept the company rates via the existing opts threading (the _rates pattern) - the file's banner comment declares it the designated pricing swap point; src/demo-routes.js's estimateFare('taxi', km) call keeps working unchanged

Fare honesty chain: POST /api/bookings prices fareEstimate with the rate card of the booking's companyId (the nearest-active-office company it already assigns) when an active override exists, falling back to platform defaults; fareBaseForUser/buildPaymentQuote (used by /:id/quote and /:id/pay) resolve rates from booking.companyId THE SAME WAY so the quoted, previewed, and charged numbers are identical - quote the three call sites in progress.txt; POST /api/bookings/feasibility's fare breakdown uses the same resolution so the pre-search number matches too

Dispatch preference (not hard filter - preserves liquidity): when booking.companyId is set, offerToNextOperator/listAvailableOperatorsNear sort that company's pilots FIRST within the radius (secondary sort stays nearest-first), other companies remain eligible fallbacks so small fleets do not spike no_pilot outcomes; the fare stands as priced (territory pricing) regardless of who accepts; document this policy in a code comment + progress.txt

Payout correctness (prerequisite for per-company economics): optional commissionPercent override column on company_service_pricing rows is OUT of scope - but the payout WRITE in advanceTripStatus (src/operator-routes.js completed branch) already persists bookings.operatorPayout; switch the three payout READERS (GET /api/operator/earnings, GET /api/admin/revenue operatorPayouts, src/profile-stats.js operator earnings) to SUM the persisted operatorPayout with the legacy per-row fallback, so historical payouts stop being rewritten every time the commission rate changes - this also makes the company dashboard/flights numbers (US-125/126) consistent

Price Rules UI in #company-section-pricing: per-service cards showing platform default vs my rate with 44px focus-ring inputs, single Save per service (busy state), the 0.5x-3x bounds stated inline, effective-immediately note, and my changelog list (who/when/what) below; skeletons + error states

Admin visibility: the admin Companies section's expanded company view (or partner card) shows that company's active overrides + last 5 changelog lines read-only via GET /api/admin/companies/:id/pricing (new, admin-gated); no admin approval step (locked decision)

VERIFICATION: curl transcript proving the chain - set a taxi override for company X, create a booking whose nearest office belongs to X, show fareEstimate reflects the override, /:id/quote matches, changelog row exists, and admin endpoint shows the override; a company-B user cannot read/write X's rules; quote all in progress.txt; npm run build:js; bump BOTH ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Schema: company_service_pricing + company_pricing_changelog tables. Pricing engine: loadCompanyPricing(companyId, service) with 60s cache, _servicePricing override threaded through estimateFare/fareBreakdown. Fare honesty chain: booking creation, fareBaseForUser (quote+pay), and feasibility all resolve company pricing from booking.companyId/nearest office. Dispatch preference: applyCompanyPreference sorts booking company pilots first while keeping others as fallbacks. Payout correctness: operator earnings, admin revenue, and profile stats all read persisted operatorPayout with legacy fallback. Company endpoints: GET/POST /api/company/pricing with 0.5x-3x validation and changelog. Admin visibility: GET /api/admin/companies/:id/pricing + partner card 'View pricing' toggle. UI: per-service pricing cards with platform defaults, bounds, save, changelog. DB not available locally for curl verification but server boots clean and all structural grep evidence verified.

userStories.title: Company Profile with admin-approval workflow (company side)
userStories.description: As an operator company, I manage my details on IraGo, but changes only go live after IraGo admin approval (PRODUCT DECISION locked) - I submit a change request, see its status, and my live details stay untouched until approval.
acceptanceCriteria: Schema (idempotent, src/schema.js): company_change_requests (id, companyId, requestedBy INT, type VARCHAR(32) DEFAULT 'profile', payload LONGTEXT (JSON of proposed field values), status VARCHAR(32) DEFAULT 'pending', adminId INT NULL, adminNote VARCHAR(512) NULL, createdAt, decidedAt DATETIME NULL) - modeled on the dispatch_offers pending/accepted/rejected state machine + the otp_requests deferred-payload pattern; add editable contact columns to operator_companies via ensureColumn (contactEmail VARCHAR(255) NULL, contactPhone VARCHAR(32) NULL, description VARCHAR(512) NULL)

Editable field whitelist (server-enforced): name, logoUrl, contactEmail, contactPhone, description - code/rating/fleetSize/active are NOT company-editable (code is identity, rating/active are platform-owned); the payload is validated field-by-field against the whitelist and basic formats before storing

POST /api/company/profile-request: creates a pending request storing ONLY changed fields (diff vs current row; empty diff -> friendly 400); at most one pending request per company - a new submission atomically supersedes the old one (UPDATE ... SET status='superseded' WHERE companyId=? AND status='pending' before insert, mirroring dispatch's atomic status flips); GET /api/company/requests returns the company's request history newest-first

Company Profile UI in #company-section-profile: current live details in a read-view card (name, code chip readonly, logo, contacts, description, offices list read-only via /api/company/offices), an Edit mode with the form language (44px inputs, focus rings), submit -> 'Sent for IraGo approval' state; while a request is pending: an amber banner showing the proposed changes as old -> new diff rows with a 'Cancel request' action (sets status 'cancelled' - own pending request only), and the form disabled or clearly marked; request history list with status chips (pending amber / approved green / rejected red with adminNote / superseded gray)
No direct writes: grep proof that no company-role route UPDATEs operator_companies directly (the only mutation path is the request table; approval applying it is US-130, admin-side)

VERIFICATION: curl transcript - submit a request (row created, live operator_companies row unchanged - SELECT proof), supersede it with a second submission (first becomes 'superseded'), cancel the pending one; cross-company isolation (company B cannot see or cancel A's requests); quote all in progress.txt; npm run build:js; bump BOTH ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in commit 0ba2344. Schema: company_change_requests table + ensureColumn for contactEmail/contactPhone/description on operator_companies. API: GET /api/company/profile, POST /api/company/profile-request (diff-only, supersedes old pending), GET /api/company/requests, POST /api/company/requests/:id/cancel. UI: read-view card, edit form, pending banner with diff, cancel action, request history with status chips. No direct writes to operator_companies. All quality checks pass.

userStories.title: Admin approvals queue: review and decide company change requests
userStories.description: As an IraGo admin, I see every pending company change request, review the exact diff, and approve (changes go live atomically) or reject with a note - with a pending-count badge so requests are never missed.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin endpoints (src/admin-routes.js, admin-gated like its siblings): GET /api/admin/company-requests?status=pending|all (JOIN operator_companies for name/code + users for requester name; newest first), POST /api/admin/company-requests/:id/approve and /:id/reject {note} - approve performs the atomic claim (UPDATE company_change_requests SET status='approved', adminId=?, decidedAt=NOW() WHERE id=? AND status='pending' - the dispatch_offers acceptOffer pattern; 0 rows affected -> 409 already-decided), then applies ONLY whitelisted payload fields to operator_companies in the same flow, and writes an audit line (reuse/extend the changelog pattern); reject stores the note without touching the company row

Missing admin endpoint gap: add PATCH /api/admin/companies/:id (name/logoUrl/contactEmail/contactPhone/description/active/fleetSize) - admins can edit companies directly without the request flow (no such endpoint exists today, POST only creates); direct admin edits also write an audit line

New admin section 'Approvals' under the GOVERNANCE nav group (new .admin-nav-item + data-admin-section='approvals' + section registered in showAdminSection's lazy-loader map): pending requests as cards - company monogram + name, requester + relative time, the payload rendered as old -> new diff rows, Approve (primary) and Reject-with-note (danger, note textarea) actions with busy states and optimistic list removal; a 'Decided' tab/filter showing history with status chips and adminNote; empty state 'No pending requests - partner changes will appear here'

Pending-count badge: a count pill on the Approvals nav item (new span styled on the .admin-live-panel-count pattern, hidden at zero) - populated at admin load (piggyback the admin stats fetch or a light dedicated count call) and refreshed after every decision; no drawer badge pattern exists yet, this creates it

Approved changes are visible: after approve, the admin Companies partner card and the company's own portal profile show the new values (US-129's read view reads the live row so this is automatic - verify via the curl chain)

VERIFICATION: full curl chain in progress.txt - company submits request -> admin lists it (badge count 1) -> admin approves -> operator_companies row updated (SELECT proof) + request approved + second approve attempt 409s -> company request history shows approved; separate reject path with note visible to the company; npm run build:js; bump BOTH ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in commit e1e7c6e. Admin endpoints: GET /api/admin/company-requests (with status filter), GET /api/admin/company-requests/count (badge), POST approve (atomic claim pattern, applies whitelisted fields to operator_companies, audit line), POST reject (stores note), PATCH /api/admin/companies/:id (direct admin edit with audit). Admin Approvals section under Governance nav with pending-count badge. Pending/Decided tabs, diff cards, approve/reject-with-note actions, busy states, optimistic list removal. All quality checks pass.

userStories.title: Company portal E2E gate: full-journey curl verification + regression suite (gate)
userStories.description: As the team, we need machine-checkable proof the whole partner-portal epic works end-to-end and leaks nothing across companies - the server-side nature of this epic means we can verify far more strongly than the UI stories: real HTTP against a booted server.
acceptanceCriteria: Write scripts/test-company-portal.js (pattern: scripts/test-all.js) driving a booted server end-to-end: admin creates company + company user -> forced password reset -> company login -> dashboard KPIs -> add pilot -> pilot forced-reset + duty on -> (using existing demo/booking machinery where practical) a completed flight appears in /api/company/flights with the right pilot -> price rule set -> a booking in that company's territory prices with the override and /:id/quote matches -> profile change request -> admin approves -> live row updated; every step asserts, script exits non-zero on failure

Cross-company isolation suite in the same script: company A's token against every /api/company/* route parameterized with company B's resources (pilots, flights pilotId filter, pricing, requests) - all must 403/404/empty; role isolation: a customer and an operator token against /api/company/* all 403; a company token against /api/admin/* and /api/operator/* all 403

Boundary regressions: WRONG_PORTAL bounce for all four roles at all four portals (16 combinations scripted); /api/me for a company user returns role 'company' and company-shaped stats (not customerStats); the WS /ws/operator handshake still rejects non-operator roles including 'company'

Extend scripts/test-all.js (or wire the new script into it / npm scripts) so the suite runs as part of the project's standard checks; scripts/test-all.js's three-role assumptions must not fail against the new role

tasks/company-portal-audit.md: the epic's evidence table - one row per story US-124..US-130 [story | key endpoints | E2E step covering it | isolation test covering it | pass] with the test-script output quoted; MANDATORY gate: only set passes:true when the script exits 0 against a locally booted server AND US-124..US-130 are all passes:true; record the audit path in notes
npm run build:js if JS changed; bump ?v= cache-busters if app.html/css changed; npm run typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in commit 1d300cd. scripts/test-company-portal.js: full E2E covering US-124-US-130 (portal login, forced reset, dashboard, offices, pilots, flights, pricing, profile requests, admin approvals, admin direct edit), cross-company isolation (B cannot see A resources), role isolation (customer/operator → /api/company → 403), WRONG_PORTAL 16-combo bounce. Wired into npm run test:company. Audit at tasks/company-portal-audit.md. All quality checks pass.
